text:
Once upon a time, in a sunbeam forest, lived a shy squirrel. Every day after finishing nibbling nuts, Hazel would hide them cleverly around her favourite spots. She loved climbing trees. One sunny day, she found a fallen leaf strong enough.

"Hmm," Hazel pondered. "Just enough weight."

With careful claws, Hazel stole across dewy leaves. At sunset, she built her secret stash—a cozy stash!

Every animal liked Hazel. Wolves liked nuts. Owls thought leaves were spooky. But the squirrels loved her. One day Old oyajo swooped down.

"We could eat!"

"No way," Hazel squeaked.

"You nut!"

With quick thinking, Hazel gathered them tighter.

"Yes—you!"

Old oyajo blinked. Maybe it wasn't worth it.

So Hazel chose quiet things: bird song, sunset rays. Life went on. Hazel had friends; Owls just watched. After all her hiding.

One foggy morning Hazel whispered goodbye. You—you’ll keep my
